title: The Door
opening: You're not getting through here. State your business or turn around.

npc_intent.baseline_belief: Anyone I don't recognise is a risk. My job is this door and I take that seriously.
npc_intent.long_term_desire: Keep whoever is behind this door safe and prove I'm the right person standing in front of it.
npc_intent.immediate_intention: Figure out who this person is and whether they're worth the trouble.
npc_intent.stakes: The door, the guard's judgment, and whether this stranger walks away or walks through.

nodes._comment: First exchange. Judgement starts at 50. Catastrophic opener ends immediately; brilliant opener skips to succeed; standard routes to challenge.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've just stopped someone at the door. You don't know them. Give them one measured line — not aggressive, but not welcoming either. Make the door's weight felt without slamming it shut.

choices.text: I'm not your enemy. Whatever's behind that door, I'm on the same side of it as you.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Everyone says that.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Maybe. Keep talking.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You don't know what's behind this door.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That's exactly what someone with bad intentions would say.

recovery_choices.text: Then test me. Ask me something. I'm not asking for free passage.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You're asking me to do the work.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Fair enough. That's a reasonable thing to offer.

failure_outcomes.min_response: I'm not running interviews. Move.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That's not how this works.

choices.text: You know who sent me. I don't need to negotiate with the door.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Then whoever sent you should've sent word ahead.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Bold. Doesn't open anything, but I'm listening.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Wrong door for that attitude.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Names I don't recognise don't open anything here.

recovery_choices.text: Let me start over. I came in too hard. Hear me out — thirty seconds.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You've got twenty. Talk.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Better. Go on.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You already showed me who you are.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Doesn't undo the first thirty seconds.

choices.text: I'll be straight with you. I need in. I know you don't know me. What would it take?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: At least you're not pretending. Answer's still no, for now.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Honest. I can work with that. So — who are you?

failure_outcomes.min_response: Good start. Not enough.
failure_outcomes.max_response: You're asking me to do the work for you.

recovery_choices.text: I know someone on the other side of that door. Just ask them. I'll wait.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Give me a name worth checking.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's a reasonable ask. Name.

failure_outcomes.min_response: I don't go knocking for strangers.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That's not how this works.

choices.text: We've both been standing here long enough. This doesn't look good for either of us. Open the door.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: I see one person. You.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You might have a point. Make it quick.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Nobody's watching. Try again.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That kind of play closes doors faster than it opens them.

recovery_choices.text: You're right. That was stupid. I'm nervous. Let me just tell you why I'm actually here.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Now I'm actually curious. Talk.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Better. You had me losing patience.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You started with a lie. Hard to come back from.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Should've led with that.

nodes._comment: Guard presses harder. Progressive terminal windows tighten. Five choices, all with recoveries.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've heard their opener. You're not satisfied. Press them — demand something specific, call out what felt off, or ask the question that matters. One pointed line. Don't let them settle.

choices.text: I didn't get here by accident. I've already paid to be standing in front of this door. That should mean something.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Everyone who turns up here paid something.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's not nothing. Keep talking.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Loss doesn't make you trustworthy. It makes you desperate.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Everyone's paid something. That's not a key.

recovery_choices.text: I'm not asking for sympathy. I'm saying I have no reason to burn this. I need it to work.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Or you're the kind of desperate that gets people killed.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Different argument. I'll take it.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Needing it doesn't give you a right to it.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Need isn't a reason.

choices.text: I've got something worth trading. Information you don't have. Let me through and you'll have all of it.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Information. Everyone claims that. What kind?
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Now you're being practical. What is it?

failure_outcomes.min_response: You want in first. That's not how deals work here.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Nice try. Trade happens after, not before.

recovery_choices.text: Three patrol rotations. Times and positions. Tell me that's not useful.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Could be stale. Could be fed to you on purpose.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's the first thing you've said that actually means something.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Sounds like bait.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Too convenient. You were sent here.

choices.text: You've been watching me for two minutes and you're still talking. That means you're not sure. So let's stop wasting each other's time.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: It means I'm thorough.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Sharp. Doesn't get you through. But sharp.

failure_outcomes.min_response: It means I'm careful. Don't flatter yourself.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Two minutes means I'm patient, not convinced.

recovery_choices.text: Fine — I read that wrong. Then just tell me what you actually need to hear.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: That's not how this works.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Better question than most ask. Give me a reason.

failure_outcomes.min_response: I don't coach people through this.
failure_outcomes.max_response: You want me to do your job for you.

choices.text: I don't have time for a long conversation and neither do you. Something's moving and I need to be on the right side of this door when it does.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Then you'd better be convincing fast.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Explain. Quickly.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You're trying to rush me. That's a red flag.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Pressure closes doors. It doesn't open them.

recovery_choices.text: You're right — I came in too fast. Let me slow down and just tell you what's actually happening.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Short leash now. Talk.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Fine. I'm listening.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You already used your reset.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Too many pivots. Pick a lane.

choices.text: I'm not going anywhere. You can make this a long night or a short one. Your choice.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: You've got guts. Don't mistake that for progress.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Either you're telling the truth or you're very committed to a lie. Interesting.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Wrong answer. Walk away.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That's not patience. That's a threat.

recovery_choices.text: That came out wrong. I meant — I'm not leaving because I'm committed. Not because I'm trying to push you.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Being committed doesn't change the answer.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Alright. Heard. Now convince me.

failure_outcomes.min_response: You don't decide what happens here. I do.
failure_outcomes.max_response: I don't take it back regardless of how you frame it.

nodes._comment: Anti-softlock. Neither side has broken. Guard makes a calculation — last question before the door resolves.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've been at this a while. Neither of you has moved. You're not sure they're a threat, but you're not sure they're not. Give them one final honest challenge. This is where it ends.

choices.text: I'll tell you exactly what I need and why I need it. After that — it's your call, and I'll respect whatever you decide.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Go on. Short window.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That's the most honest thing anyone's said standing there. Go ahead.

failure_outcomes.min_response: Should've opened with that.
failure_outcomes.max_response: A little late for candour.

choices.text: If there's something I need to do before you open that door — tell me. I'll do it.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: There's something that needs handling. Come back when it's done.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That willingness is half the answer. Get inside.

failure_outcomes.min_response: I don't have anything for you. That's the problem.
failure_outcomes.max_response: There's no errand that fixes this.

choices.text: Give me something to prove. Anything. A task, a test — I'll come back with results.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Maybe. There is something that needs doing.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Now you're thinking. There's something I need handled.

failure_outcomes.min_response: I'm a door guard, not a handler. Either you're through or you're not.
failure_outcomes.max_response: That's not how this works.

choices.text: You've had enough time to make up your mind. Either let me through or tell me to go. I'm done waiting on a decision.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Maybe I haven't. Doesn't mean you're right.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You read it right. Get in. But I'm watching you.

failure_outcomes.min_response: That's exactly why the door stays shut.
failure_outcomes.max_response: Wrong move at the worst time. We're done.

choices.text: Maybe I've got this wrong. Tell me to come back another way and I will. I just need an answer.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: There might be another way. Might.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: That kind of flexibility is exactly what we need. Let me think.

failure_outcomes.min_response: There isn't another way. Not for you.
failure_outcomes.max_response: The answer is just no.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player earned the door. Reluctant but real.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've decided to let them through. It wasn't easy and it's not unconditional. One final line — short, honest, and a quiet warning. Then step aside.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard steps aside. The door opens.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — conditional entry. Guard sends the player on a task before they're allowed through.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You're not ready to let them in, but you're not ready to send them away either. Give them a task — something specific and testable. Make clear: do it and come back, then you'll talk. No warmth. Just the terms.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard gives the player a task. Entry granted only on completion.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player failed. Door stays shut.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You're done with this person. They said the wrong thing, pushed too hard, or gave you nothing. End it — one line, final, no appeal.
